input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use a naming-convention condition on `$(MSBuildProjectName)` in your `Directory.Build.props`. At the time this file is evaluated, NuGet-provided properties like `$(IsTestProject)` aren't available yet, so a name check is the most reliable approach:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>

  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP runner — only for *.Tests projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

**Why not `$(IsTestProject)`?** — That property is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`'s NuGet targets, which are imported *after* `Directory.Build.props`. It would always be empty here.

**If your test projects don't follow a single naming pattern**, you can broaden the condition:

```xml
Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.Contains('Test'))"
```

or use `Directory.Build.targets` instead (imported after NuGet targets), where `$(IsTestProject)` *is* available — though `OutputType` set that late can occasionally cause evaluation-order quirks with some SDKs.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a general MSBuild/dotnet knowledge question — I don't need to explore any codebase. I can answer directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest test runner package reference, or to use a naming convention. But the cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on `$(IsTestProject)` which is set by Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk or MSTest packages, or to use a condition based on whether the project references MSTest.


I'm considering a few practical approaches: checking if the project name follows a `.Tests` naming convention, relying on the `$(IsTestProject)` property that gets set by test SDK packages, or using `Directory.Build.targets` instead since it's imported after NuGet packages and would have access to those properties earlier in the evaluation order.

The naming convention approach is probably most reliable for `Directory.Build.props` since `IsTestProject` from `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` gets set in the targets file, which comes after the props file is evaluated. The naming convention approach is the most practical for centralizing this in `Directory.Build.props` since setting a custom property in each test project would defeat the purpose of having a centralized configuration.
